entries.name: Magnetic chain
entries.trigger: Cursor position (continuous). No click, no scroll. Desktop only — the vocabulary loses its meaning on touch.
entries.easing: `cubic-bezier(0.25, 0.46, 0.45, 0.94)` — ease-out-quad. Calm decay; the chain settles, it doesn't snap.
entries.duration: 140ms response on each link. Chain transmission is staggered by the per-link response, not by an explicit delay.

entries.name: Paper-fold
entries.trigger: Hover or click. Never continuous, never time-driven.
entries.easing: `cubic-bezier(0.22, 1, 0.36, 1)` — out-expo. The fold accelerates into the hinge and decelerates to rest, like real paper falling.
entries.duration: 600ms hinge. Long enough to read as material, short enough not to drag.

entries.name: Rack focus
entries.trigger: Hover on any element within the grid. The grid is the trigger surface, individual elements are the foci.
entries.easing: `cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.2, 1)` — standard cinematic ease, mirrors how a camera lens actually pulls focus.
entries.duration: 300ms focus pull. Felt as a camera operation, not a screen transition.

entries.name: Iris transition
entries.trigger: Click or route change. Never hover, never scroll-into-view, never time-driven.
entries.easing: `cubic-bezier(0.65, 0, 0.35, 1)` — in-out-cubic. Symmetric closing and opening; the pause at full-closed is part of the move.
entries.duration: 1100ms total — 550ms close, 550ms open. Built for a deliberate beat, not for speed.

entries.name: Heat trace
entries.trigger: Cursor movement (continuous). Desktop only — touch has no continuous cursor.
entries.easing: Linear decay (per trail node) plus radial fade (per trail node). Each trail node fades by a constant factor per frame; the trail length is implicit in the decay rate.
entries.duration: Each trail point persists ~700ms then fades. The continuous trail is alive as long as the cursor moves.

entries.name: Type-set-on-scroll
entries.trigger: Scroll progress through the element. Each element animates only on first scroll-through; subsequent re-entries do not replay (unless an explicit "replay" handle is provided).
entries.easing: Linear, mapped to scroll progress through the element. The user controls the pace.
entries.duration: Tied to scroll dwell — approximately 1.2 seconds of read-equivalent travel through the element, but actual duration depends on the user's scroll speed.

entries.name: Chromatic separation
entries.trigger: Hover on display text only. Never on body, never on icons, never on form elements.
entries.easing: `ease-out` — the split engages quickly on hover, releases gently on un-hover. Asymmetric is correct here: the holographic moment is fast, the resolution is slow.
entries.duration: 150ms engage, ~250ms release. The hover moment feels electric; the return to neutral feels considered.

entries.name: Match-cut morph
entries.trigger: Click or tap. Never hover, never scroll, never continuous.
entries.easing: `cubic-bezier(0.22, 1, 0.36, 1)` — out-expo. The morph accelerates into the new shape and decelerates to rest. Symmetric is wrong here; the destination shape should feel like the morph's resting point.
entries.duration: 600ms shape transition. Long enough for the morph to be read as continuity, short enough not to drag.

entries.name: Drift compositions
entries.trigger: Continuous; independent per-element loops. Never coupled to user input, scroll, or events.
entries.easing: `ease-in-out` (sinusoidal) per shape. Each shape follows its own path independently of others.
entries.duration: 19–31 seconds per loop, with deliberately different periods per shape — the composition never returns to the same configuration on a predictable beat.

entries.name: Color bleed
entries.trigger: Hover. Never click, never scroll, never continuous.
entries.easing: `cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.2, 1)` for the engage; a longer linear decay on release. Asymmetric is correct here: the bleed is fast, the recede is slow — like real ink soaking into paper and then evaporating.
entries.duration: 400ms engage on hover, 800ms recede on un-hover. The asymmetry between engage and release is essential to the metaphor.

entries.name: Crosshatching reveal
entries.trigger: Scroll-into-view, once per element. Never replays, never on hover, never continuous.
entries.easing: `cubic-bezier(0.65, 0, 0.35, 1)` — symmetric in-out-cubic. Stripes draw apart along the same curve they would close on, like a deliberate two-hand gesture.
entries.duration: 1400ms total reveal, with each stripe staggered 80ms. Long enough that the engraving metaphor reads; short enough not to delay first-content-visible past the user's patience.
